scope_note: A formal political party or party organization.
use_when: Use for parties such as communist parties, ruling parties, opposition parties, or party delegations when the source treats them as party organizations.
do_not_use_when: Do not use for states, governments, diplomatic blocs, or broad ideological camps.
examples: Chinese Communist Party; Kuomintang; political party delegations
notes: May require adding party entities/organizations later; currently not heavily used in the core organization vocabulary.

scope_note: A government claiming state authority while operating outside the territory it claims or without full territorial control.
use_when: Use for records explicitly referring to a government-in-exile or similar exile government structure.
do_not_use_when: Do not use merely for opposition movements, liberation movements, or governments not recognized by Japan unless exile status is explicit.
examples: Government-in-exile; exile cabinet; provisional government abroad
notes: Potentially relevant to Cambodia, Vietnam, or liberation movement records if source wording supports it.

scope_note: A movement claiming national liberation, anti-colonial struggle, or self-determination.
use_when: Use for named liberation movements, fronts, or organizations when source treats them as national liberation actors.
do_not_use_when: Do not use for all newly independent states or all anti-colonial topics without a named movement actor.
notes: Can coexist with armed_movement if the source emphasizes armed struggle.

scope_note: A coalition/front organization uniting multiple parties, movements, or groups under a common political cause.
use_when: Use for explicit united fronts, national united fronts, or front governments/movements.
do_not_use_when: Do not use for general diplomatic coalitions or conference groupings unless they are organized as a front.
examples: National United Front; united front organization; front government
notes: May overlap with national_liberation_movement; preserve source wording in original_terms.

scope_note: A non-state or quasi-state movement using armed struggle as a defining feature.
use_when: Use when the source explicitly treats the actor as an armed movement, guerrilla organization, insurgent movement, or military-political organization.
do_not_use_when: Do not use for regular state militaries or military alliances.
examples: guerrilla movement; insurgent front; armed liberation movement
notes: Can coexist conceptually with national_liberation_movement, but use source evidence.

scope_note: A formal intergovernmental or international organization, including UN bodies and regional organizations.
use_when: Use for UN, UN organs, specialized agencies, regional organizations, and formal international institutions.
do_not_use_when: Do not use for informal blocs, negotiating groups, or movements unless they are institutionalized organizations.
examples: UN; UNGA; UNCTAD; OAU; ASEAN; League of Arab States
notes: This is a broad normalized type. Keep detailed legacy organization_type text for specificity.

preferred_label_jp: 交渉連合／交渉グループ
scope_note: A coalition or group formed for negotiation, voting, or conference diplomacy.
use_when: Use only when the actor is treated as a relatively organized negotiating coalition or group, especially in multilateral conference contexts.
do_not_use_when: Do not use for conceptual blocs or regional categories unless the source identifies a negotiating group. Many cases belong in bloc_ids rather than organization_ids.
examples: Group of 77; UNCTAD Group B; African Group; Asian Group
notes: Often better represented as bloc_id in this database. Use as organization_type_id only if a separate organization authority record is created for the group.
